item.title: [Segurança] CVE-2026-3614 - Check Point
item.description:
🚨 CVE-2026-3614 - Check Point
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Check Point
Fonte: NVD
CVSS: 8.8
Resumo:
The AcyMailing plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to privilege escalation in all versions From 9.11.0 up to, and including, 10.8.1 due to a missing capability check on the `wp_ajax_acymailing_router` AJAX handler. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to access admin-only controllers (including configuration management), enable the autologin feature, create a mali...
Link: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-3614

item.title: [Segurança] Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
item.description:
🚨 Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Qualys
Fonte: The Hacker News
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
A publicação relata a vulnerabilidade CVE-2026-34197 associada a Qualys. Vale consultar o link para verificar impacto, versões afetadas e orientações de correção.
Link: https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/apache-activemq-cve-2026-34197-added-to.html

item.title: [Sistemas / Cloud] CVE-2026-32631 - Windows Server
item.description:
🚨 CVE-2026-32631 - Windows Server
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Windows Server
Fonte: NVD
CVSS: 7.4
Resumo:
Versions prior to 2.53.0.windows.3 do not have protections that prevent attackers from obtaining a user's NTLM hash. The NTLM hash can be obtained by tricking users into cloning a malicious repository, or checking out a malicious branch, that accesses an attacker-controlled server. By default, NTLM authentication does not need any user interaction. By brute-forcing the NTLMv2 hash (which is expensive, but possible),...
Link: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-32631

item.title: [Sistemas / Cloud] USN-8184-1: Linux kernel (Real-time) vulnerabilities
item.description:
🚨 USN-8184-1: Linux kernel (Real-time) vulnerabilities
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Ubuntu
Fonte: https://ubuntu.com/security/notices/rss.xml
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
Josh Eads, Kristoffer Janke, Eduardo Vela Nava, Tavis Ormandy, and Matteo Rizzo discovered that some AMD Zen processors did not properly verify the signature of CPU microcode. A privileged attacker could possibly use this issue to cause load malicious CPU microcode causing loss of integrity and confidentiality. (CVE-2024-36347) Several security issues were discovered in the Linux kernel. An attacker could possibly us...
Link: https://ubuntu.com/security/notices/USN-8184-1

item.title: [Sistemas / Cloud] USN-8188-1: Linux kernel (HWE) vulnerabilities
item.description:
🚨 USN-8188-1: Linux kernel (HWE) vulnerabilities
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Ubuntu
Fonte: https://ubuntu.com/security/notices/rss.xml
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
Several security issues were discovered in the Linux kernel. An attacker could possibly use these to compromise the system. This update corrects flaws in the following subsystems: - ARM64 architecture; - Block layer subsystem; - Cryptographic API; - Drivers core; - Bluetooth drivers; - DMA engine subsystem; - GPU drivers; - HID subsystem; - Intel Trace Hub HW tracing drivers; - IIO ADC drivers; - IRQ chip drivers; -...
Link: https://ubuntu.com/security/notices/USN-8188-1

item.title: [Sistemas / Cloud] CVE-2026-4949 - WordPress
item.description:
🚨 CVE-2026-4949 - WordPress
 
Criticidade: Média
Ferramenta: WordPress
Fonte: NVD
CVSS: 4.3
Resumo:
The Paid Membership Plugin, Ecommerce, User Registration Form, Login Form, User Profile & Restrict Content – ProfilePress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in all versions up to, and including, 4.16.12. This is due to the 'process_checkout' function not properly enforcing the plan active status check when a 'change_plan_sub_id' parameter is provided. This makes it possible for authenticated...
Link: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-4949

item.title: [Infraestrutura] CVE-2025-12141 - Grafana
item.description:
🚨 CVE-2025-12141 - Grafana
 
Criticidade: Média
Ferramenta: Grafana
Fonte: NVD
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
In Grafana's alerting system, users with edit permissions for a contact point, specifically the permissions “alert.notifications:write” or “alert.notifications.receivers:test” that are granted as part of the fixed role "Contact Point Writer", which is part of the basic role Editor - can edit contact points created by other users, modify the endpoint URL to a controlled server. By invoking the test functionality, atta...
Link: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-12141

item.title: [Segurança] CISA flags Apache ActiveMQ flaw as actively exploited in attacks
item.description:
💣 CISA flags Apache ActiveMQ flaw as actively exploited in attacks
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: SentinelOne
Fonte: BleepingComputer
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
CISA warned that attackers are now exploiting a high-severity Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability, which was patched earlier this month after going undetected for 13 years.
Link: https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/cisa-flags-apache-activemq-flaw-as-actively-exploited-in-attacks/

item.title: [Segurança] Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
item.description:
💣 Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Qualys
Fonte: The Hacker News
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. The activity involves the exploitation of three vulnerabilities that are codenamed BlueHammer (requires GitHub sign-in), RedSun, and UnDefend, all of which were released as zero-days by a researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse (
Link: https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/three-microsoft-defender-zero-days.html

Fonte: BleepingComputer
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:

item.title: [Segurança] Hackers exploit Marimo flaw to deploy NKAbuse malware from Hugging Face
item.description:
💣 Hackers exploit Marimo flaw to deploy NKAbuse malware from Hugging Face
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: SentinelOne
Fonte: BleepingComputer
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
Hackers are exploiting a critical vulnerability in Marimo reactive Python notebook to deploy a new variant of NKAbuse malware hosted on Hugging Face Spaces.
Link: https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/hackers-exploit-marimo-flaw-to-deploy-nkabuse-malware-from-hugging-face/

item.title: [Infraestrutura] Converging VMware vSphere to VMware Cloud Foundation 9.0: The Top 10 Questions Answered
item.description:
📰 Converging VMware vSphere to VMware Cloud Foundation 9.0: The Top 10 Questions Answered
 
Criticidade: Crítica
Ferramenta: ESX
Fonte: https://blogs.vmware.com/feed
CVSS: 9.0
Resumo:
Transitioning your VMware vSphere environment to VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) is a strategic move that shifts your infrastructure from a collection of managed silos to a unified private cloud. In my recent webinar with Brent Douglas where we dove deep into the converge process, the turnout was massive.
Link: https://blogs.vmware.com/cloud-foundation/2026/04/16/converging-vmware-vsphere-to-vmware-cloud-foundation-9-0-the-top-10-questions-answered/

item.title: [Segurança] NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
item.description:
📰 NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Qualys
Fonte: The Hacker News
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced changes to the way it handles cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) listed in its National Vulnerability Database (NVD), stating it will only enrich those that fulfil certain conditions owing to an explosion in CVE submissions. "CVEs that do not meet those criteria will still be listed in the NVD but will not
Link: https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/nist-limits-cve-enrichment-after-263.html

item.title: [Infraestrutura] Why AI won’t steal your SOC analyst job
item.description:
📰 Why AI won’t steal your SOC analyst job
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Elastic
Fonte: https://www.elastic.co/blog/feed
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
AI won't replace SOC analysts but will change the job. AI automates repetitive tasks; humans provide critical context, creativity, and judgment for strategic security. Learn more about what the future looks like for the SOC analyst with AI.
Link: https://www.elastic.co/blog/why-ai-will-not-steal-your-soc-analyst-job
